title: Form Item
description: The form item type: a container-like type that groups variable-widget children and arranges them via one of two MODES selected by the `layout.mode` discriminator. In `flow` mode (E2-S1) widgets pack into compact label+control cells (optionally split into N columns) and never declare or consume a main-grid placement. In `grid` mode (E2-S2) the form arranges its widget children on a weighted grid identical in shape to `container` — relative-weight column/row track lists plus a relative gap — and each child carries an explicit 1-indexed `placement`; the resolver reuses the SAME layout.Block path as `container`, so placement bounds reuse LAYOUT_PLACEMENT_*. Like container, form is structurally special — it may carry `children` — but flow and grid are two modes of the one `form` type, not two types. A form rejects non-widget children fail-fast (LAYOUT_FORM_CHILD_INVALID); an out-of-range flow column count fails LAYOUT_FORM_COLUMNS_INVALID. NO CSS keywords or absolute units appear anywhere — flow columns is a unitless count and grid weights are unitless relative weights.

constraints.description: The form's layout declaration (E3-S3 surface). Editable at runtime: the layout `mode` (flow vs grid) and, in flow mode, the unitless `columns` count children pack into; in grid mode the relative-weight `columns`/`rows` track lists and `gap`. All unitless and relative — NO CSS units or keywords. Declared with the array variable type per the top-level-only surface limitation (the layout is an object whose editable sub-fields are described here, matching the container `grid` surface convention).

columns.description: Flow mode: a unitless integer column count (1–12). Grid mode: a positive-number relative-weight track array.

layout.title: Form Layout
layout.description: The form's layout declaration. `mode` selects the arrangement: `flow` (compact label+control stacking, the default) or `grid` (a weighted grid identical to container's). The `columns` integer applies to flow mode; the `columns`/`rows`/`gap` weights apply to grid mode. Omit the whole object for a single-column flow.

mode.title: Layout Mode
mode.description: Discriminator selecting the form's arrangement. `flow` (the default) stacks each child into a label+control row, filling left-to-right across `columns` then wrapping. `grid` arranges children on a weighted grid identical in shape to container's, with each child placed via its explicit `placement`. Authors pick flow vs grid per form instance.

columns.title: Layout Columns
columns.description: In `flow` mode: the number of columns the flow arranges children into, as a unitless integer count (children fill row-major, then wrap; defaults to 1). In `grid` mode: relative, unitless weights for the column tracks, in left-to-right order, normalized to fractions summing to 1. The `if/then` keywords below constrain `columns` to the integer form in flow mode and the weight-array form in grid mode. NO CSS units or keywords.

rows.title: Grid Row Track Weights
rows.description: Grid mode only: relative, unitless weights for the row tracks, in top-to-bottom order. Each weight is a positive number; the resolver normalizes the list to fractions that sum to 1. Omit for a single full-height row. NO CSS units or keywords.

gap.title: Grid Track Gap
gap.description: Grid mode only: relative, unitless gap between tracks, expressed in the same relative space as the track weights. Defaults to 0 (no gap). NO CSS units or keywords.

columns.title: Grid Column Track Weights
columns.description: Grid mode: relative, unitless weights for the column tracks, in left-to-right order. Each weight is a positive number; the resolver normalizes the list to fractions that sum to 1. Omit for a single full-width column.

columns.title: Flow Columns
columns.description: Flow mode: number of columns the flow arranges children into, as a unitless count. Children fill row-major (left-to-right, then wrap). Defaults to 1 (a single stacked column).
